supersession_reason: The v2 public generator exposed only six distinct feature-label patterns and was therefore enumerable before hidden attachment.

runtime.floating_point_contract: decimal features are produced from signed integer micro-units and serialized with six fractional digits

sequence.label_rule: positive when the final latent signed context state is greater than or equal to zero, otherwise negative
sequence.candidate_visible_id_rule: opaque evaluator supplied sequence and observation identifiers

modular_generation_pipeline.purpose: sample a latent serial-context rule family, signed evidence orientation, transition gain, decay, and reset behavior
sample sequence length, evidence positions, distractor positions, delayed cue positions, and final-state margin
reject degenerate sequences, near-zero final margins, repeated canonical feature sequences, and baseline-solvable artifacts
add bounded deterministic jitter, distractor drift, and irrelevant feature perturbations without changing the latent label
emit canonical ordered unlabeled feature pairs and keep labels plus latent metadata outside the candidate boundary

acceptance_rejection_checks: Reject any generated artifact with fewer unique canonical sequences than the split-specific floor.

Reject any artifact where a majority-class, last-observation, uniform-pooling, non-neural finite-state, or parameter-matched stateless baseline exceeds the preregistered baseline ceiling before the hidden run.
Reject any artifact with train, development, or hidden canonical sequence digest overlap.
Reject any hidden artifact whose seed, labels, latent metadata, provider identity, or per-sequence correctness can enter the candidate repository, candidate process, logs, or prediction output.
Reject any post-commitment change to generator contract, candidate code, model parameters, thresholds, evaluator executable, or scoring protocol.

digest_contract.canonicalization: sorted-key compact JSON with decimal feature strings

separation_rules: The public train and development artifacts may be generated before candidate training and must be bound by digest.
The hidden seed, labels, latent variables, provider-specific implementation details, and detailed correctness remain outside the implementer trust domain.
Train, development, and hidden identifiers plus canonical sequence digests are pairwise disjoint.
The hidden provider commits the hidden artifact digest before candidate scoring begins.
The independent evaluator owns hidden scoring, baseline scoring, ablation scoring, attempt ledgers, and signed aggregate evidence.
A public generator description is not sufficient for hidden validity unless the accepted artifact satisfies the search-space and split-isolation floors.
